sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.7g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a fruit, the cleanest wins are low sugar and decent fibre.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side: quite perishable because storage life is not amazing compared with sturdier fruits. can be pricey because cost can limit how practical it is as a staple fruit. protein contribution is negligible because not a real flaw for fruit, but still basically absent.

closing.summary: Raspberries has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want sweetness that still earns its place nutritionally.
